_note: goodFor for batch-4 (20 programs, 2026-07-07). Same conventions as good-for.json/good-for-3.json. Apply with prisma/apply-good-for.ts AFTER prisma/import-researched.ts researched-programs-4.json has created these rows.

programs.goodFor: For university graduates who want pluralistic, egalitarian Torah study in seminar-style classes and chavruta pairs, with the flexibility to study for just Elul, a semester, or the full year.
For adults of any background wanting a full-time egalitarian, Conservative/Masorti-inflected yeshiva experience, from a single summer or winter session up to a full year.
For recent high school graduates who want a Jerusalem gap year split evenly between North American and Israeli peers, pairing Hartman Institute-style pluralistic text study with leadership training.
For young adults (18-21) who want to choose their own social-service focus — health, education, or welfare — alongside political and current-events education, while living independently in Tel Aviv.

For UK Jewish young adults wanting a two-city gap year — a Jerusalem semester then a Tel Aviv-area semester — with a free choice of Special Interest Month: kibbutz, yeshiva, hiking, business internship, or surfing.

For UK school leavers wanting a Socialist-Zionist, collective-living gap year — three months on kibbutz, then four months living communally while volunteering and teaching English in an Israeli city, plus a Poland trip.
For young Masorti/Conservative-movement adults from Noam's global network wanting a Galilee-based gap year built around Zionism, Masorti Judaism, tikkun olam and leadership education.

For post-high-schoolers wanting a fully supervised Maccabi-movement gap year mixing leadership seminars, a taste of IDF Marva and MDA training, kibbutz work, and touring, over a flexible 5-10 month length.
For young Israelis and diaspora peers who want to spend 6 months together in a Jewish Peoplehood leadership program, with an optional 3+ month internship extension.

For young adults wanting an elite, hands-on 10-month government/policy internship inside Israeli ministries and NGOs, paired with intensive Hebrew study and weekly access to top Israeli officials and policymakers.

For North American college-student leaders wanting an intensive 16-day Israel advocacy and leadership crash course, meeting Israeli political, military and business figures and both Israelis and Palestinians firsthand.

For recent high school graduates (3.0+ GPA) wanting a brand-new (2026-27 launch) Tel Aviv University gap year pairing for-credit coursework with cybersecurity/AI/entrepreneurship workshops and mentorship from Israeli founders.
For US/Canadian pre-med graduates (MCAT required) wanting an English-language M.D. degree in Haifa preparing them specifically to practice back in North America.
For English-speaking men wanting to combine a real yeshiva learning schedule (mornings) with an accredited Computer Science or Business Administration degree taught in English (afternoons).

For international students, Olim, and globally minded applicants wanting an English/Hebrew-hybrid Israeli degree — the International Law LLB is the flagship track — while gradually integrating into a large, multicultural campus.

For international students 18+ wanting one semester of English-taught art, design or engineering coursework at a school that fuses all three disciplines, with hands-on workshops taught in simplified Hebrew.
For religious women wanting an accredited multi-year arts, design or drama degree, with dedicated English-language coursework support built in.
For outstanding international musicians (audition required) wanting a fully-funded Bachelor's, Master's, or Artist Diploma studying under Israel Philharmonic principal players in Tel Aviv.
For lacrosse players (18-30) wanting a gap year built around competing and coaching in Israel — training, league games, European tournaments, and mentoring local youth teams as sport ambassadors.
For unaffiliated, college-educated professional women (22-30) wanting a flexible-length, no-background-assumed introduction to Torah Judaism designed specifically for women exploring observance.
